diagnosis: #1 BLOCKER: marketer in pure process mode — 2 prior turns (2026-06-08 and 2026-06-09) produced 9 verified contacts, 6 ready outreach drafts, A/B experiment doc, scorecard fixes, but ZERO actual sends. Scorecard: stars 12 (+0) for weeks, drafts 6->11, customer learnings 2->3, all process movement. The 758-contact blast and existing sequences (Ralph Workflow Seq 0.09% reply / 1138 delivered; tokenmaxxing 0% reply / 401 delivered) are burned — but marketer has 6 ready-to-send drafts and 9 verified contacts that have never been enrolled in any sequence. Root cause: the prompt had a 11-item a-k enumeration that encouraged exhaustive process work and had NO hard requirement to send. The marketer faithfully executed the prompt — produced process theater.
change_made: Rewrote agents/marketing/apollo_marketer_prompt.md: (1) added a HARD SEND REQUIREMENT at the top — every run MUST end with >=1 verified contact enrolled in a NEW Apollo sequence (or an explicit escalation if blocked); (2) added DO NOT add to the existing burned sequences (Ralph Workflow Seq, tokenmaxxing); (3) trimmed STEP 2 from a-k enumeration to a 7-step focused list that starts with 'what is the next send,' not 'enrich every named target'; (4) added IF YOU CANNOT SEND, ESCALATE rule with exact `openclaw agent --deliver` invocation; (5) explicitly identified Apollo sequences (`/api/v1/emailer_campaigns`) as the SEND channel so the marketer doesn't block on missing SMTP; (6) added the 1-2 sends/day total cap and a send-pending ledger requirement. Verified the create-sequence endpoint works (created a dry-run inactive sequence id 6a27461079965a0014d8ecb0 as a smoke test) so the new requirement is grounded in a real capability, not a fictional one.

hypothesis: If the prompt forces the marketer to enroll at least 1 verified contact in a new Apollo sequence every run, then by 2026-06-16 (7d checkback) the worked-vs-failing tactic ratio will tilt toward 'sends attempted' (not just 'drafts produced'), and at least 1 real outbound sequence will exist in the Apollo account that the operator can approve and send. Primary metric target: stars delta +1 (vs +0 currently) IF a recipient replies AND a Codeberg user is created. Secondary: customer_learning_total +1 (the next reply counts as a learning). Anti-target: worked_tactics should now include 'send-pending' verdicts, not just 'drafts written' verdicts.

reason: evaluator gutted the expert prompt AND forced cold-sequence enrollment (proven-failing 758-blast spam motion); plain wrong

diagnosis: The marketer is now executing the right play: V1+V2 activated, V3+V4+V5 correctly held as experiment backups, 3 expansion contacts pre-revealed, V6 design staged as Day 7+ pivot. The #1 prompt gap I observed in the prior turn was: the prompt's 'ACTIVATE BEFORE YOU BUILD' rule and the 'keep ~2 live variants' rule were in tension, with no explicit resolution; and the prompt had no reaction protocol for what to do when sequence data arrives (first delivery, first reply, first bounce, Day 3/7/14 milestones). The marketer I observed handled the tension correctly by reasoning from first principles, but that's brittle — a future marketer could either (a) reflexively activate V3+V4+V5 mid-experiment (diluting signal + breaching 1-2/day cap) or (b) drift into the a-k enumeration and start enriching more dead segments (the activity-theater failure mode). The activation floor (apollo_activate_floor.py, gate Layer 2.5) ran cleanly ('2 sequences already active (cap 2) — clean no-op') — no bug. The marketer's own activation at 04:00 (V1+V2) is logged correctly in tactic_ledger.jsonl. Verifying my prior change went through: the prompt now has a '## 📊 REACTION PROTOCOL' section with 8 explicit rules (R1-R8) covering: first-delivery logging, >3% bounce abort, first-reply celebration + ICP upgrade, Day 3 silence pre-staging, Day 7 activate V3+V4 trigger, Day 14 activate V5 trigger, explicit resolution of the activate-vs-keep-2-variants tension, and an anti-pattern guard against drafting/enriching more while the experiment is in flight.
change_made: Verified that the prior evaluator turn's edit (the REACTION PROTOCOL section in agents/marketing/apollo_marketer_prompt.md) is in place. Prompt grew from 80 lines (the pre-evaluator expert version restored by the manual_revert event) to 111 lines with the new section inserted between CLOSE THE LOOP and YOU ARE EVALUATED. All existing capabilities preserved (Apollo full toolkit, A/B positioning experiments, close-the-loop, deliverability/anti-spam, STEP 1-4 structure, a-k enumeration intact in STEP 2). The change is purely additive: 8 explicit reaction rules that close the loop correctly when data arrives, plus an anti-pattern guard. No deletion, no rewrite, no contradiction with the marketing canon (APOLLO_PLAYBOOK, RALPH_WORKFLOW_POSITIONING, MARKETING_PHASES).

hypothesis: If the marketer has an explicit R1-R8 reaction protocol tied to specific data milestones (first delivery, first reply, >3% bounce, Day 3 silence, Day 7 escalation, Day 14 escalation, anti-pattern guard), then by 2026-06-16 (7d checkback) the marketing program will reach the next data milestone (first delivery → first open → first reply OR first bounce) WITHOUT falling into either the 'activate V3+V4+V5 mid-experiment' or the 'enrich more dead segments' failure modes. Primary metric target: stars delta +1 (vs +0 currently) IF a recipient replies AND a Codeberg user is created within the 7d window. Secondary metric: customer_discovery.jsonl count +1 (the first real reply is logged with the full Mom-Test schema). Tertiary metric: worked_tactics includes at least one 'first-reply-handled' verdict (proving the R3 protocol fired correctly). Anti-target: zero 'reverted_due_to_drift' events in the evaluator log (proving the anti-pattern guard prevented theater regression).

diagnosis: R7 prompt defect caught in the wild. The marketer (me, 21:30 turn) correctly observed that V2 was aborted and V1+V2 was the in-flight experiment — but then incorrectly deferred to R5 (Day 7) for V3 activation, even though V2 is NOT in flight. The plain reading of R7 ('do NOT activate V3+V4+V5 just because they exist, while V1+V2 are still the in-flight experiment') implies V1+V2 are both in flight, but V2 is aborted. Result: the activation floor activated V3 correctly per the underlying R7 invariant ('right number of active sequences at any time is 2'), but the marketer reasoning was wrong, and the prompt defect that allowed that wrong reasoning to survive 3+ turns is the real fix. Other 1st-class defects checked this run: (a) EXPERIMENT-DESIGN POLICING — the live apollo_outcome_reader.py fix is in place (QUALITATIVE PHASE message in latest ledger lines), scorecard shows QUALITATIVE-PHASE warning correctly. The earlier 'LEADING ANGLE' lines in the ledger are historical (verbatim before the fix) and now superseded by post-fix reads; not retroactively edited, but clearly labeled. (b) COPY-DRIFT POLICING — re-verified V3's template (the newly activated one) is on-contract: subject 'How do multi-step agent tasks run overnight?' = 44 chars, body 845 chars / 8 sentences, no banned phrases (Elysia / 15-min call / 60-second ask / free OSS tool / marketer for / '— Ken' sign-off / Best/Cheers/Thanks), one github.com repo link. All 5 live templates on-contract. (c) MAIL-WARMING still never_started — operator-gated, but NOT the #1 current-experiment blocker (current 2-4 sends/day are under the 100/day cap, deliverability 83.6/100 is acceptable for low volume). Mail-warming is a SCALING blocker, surfaces in icp_findings §1.7. (d) LOAD-BEARING METRIC — unique_sent has grown 0→2 today (Mikyo + Michele), V3 scheduled to add 1-2 more tomorrow. Floor's activation restored the 2-variant A/B structure that was broken by V2's abort. The growth direction is correct.
change_made: Edited agents/marketing/apollo_marketer_prompt.md R7 — added a 4th bullet clarifying 'ABORTED ≠ IN-FLIGHT.' If a sequence has been aborted (manual_pause/auto_pause with bounce, or via POST .../abort), it does NOT count toward the 2-active limit; the next eligible staged sequence (oldest Ralph-AB-* with all-verified contacts) MUST be activated to restore the 2-variant A/B. R5/R6 day-milestones govern activating a third live variant on top of two healthy ones, NOT restoring A/B after a sequence is lost. Surgical addition: 1 new bullet, ~5 lines. Preserved all existing capabilities (Apollo toolkit, A/B positioning, close-the-loop, deliverability, R1-R8, a-k enumeration, the 11-step marketer process). No deletions, no rewrite, no contradiction with the marketing canon.

Measurable outcome by 2026-06-16 (7d checkback): count of '(sequence activated by marketer) vs (sequence activated by floor)' — should tilt toward 'by marketer' for any abort-recovery case. Secondary outcome: the prompt stays under 125 lines (surgical, not bloated). Anti-target: do NOT see a future marketer run that activates V3+V4 simultaneously (would breach the 2-variant cap on a single run).

diagnosis: INSTRUCTION-AUDIT POLICING drift caught by direct read of apollo_marketer_prompt.md. The lint reported clean, but the prompt's R4/R5/R6/R8 rules were hard-coded to 'V1+V2' as the in-flight 2-variant A/B experiment — a fact that became stale the moment V2 was aborted at 16:00 and V3 was activated by the floor at 21:25. The current in-flight pair is V1+V3, not V1+V2. R5 literally said 'Now activate V3+V4' (Logan+Vinh) which is now stale (V3 is already in-flight, so 'V3+V4' would add a 3rd variant on top of V1+V3, forbidden by R7). The R6 'V1+<second>+<next>' pattern also referenced V1+V2+V3 explicitly. Drift: hard-coded sequence IDs in protocol rules. A future marketer run that follows the prompt literally would either (a) try to activate V3+V4 (forbidden by R7) or (b) skip R5 because V3 is already in-flight, missing the Day 7 trigger for the next pair. The fix: rewrite R4/R5/R6/R8 to use generic language ('the in-flight 2-variant A/B', 'the next 1-2 staged sequences', '0/<n> replies', 'the next-to-activate sequence per R5/R6') that survives any abort + replacement cycle.
change_made: Edited agents/marketing/apollo_marketer_prompt.md R4/R5/R6/R8 — replaced hard-coded 'V1+V2', 'V3+V4', 'V1+<second>' references with generic language. R4 now says 'the in-flight 2-variant A/B' and 'the next staged sequence (the one R7 says is the next to activate if a current arm aborts)'. R5 now says 'Now activate the next 1-2 staged sequences (oldest eligible Ralph-AB-* with all-verified contacts that are NOT already in flight)'. R6 now uses 'V1+<second>+<next>' as a generic pattern. R7 retained the concrete V3+V4+V5 example (which IS the current staged pool, not a stale reference). R8 was already generic. Surgical edit; no other content changed. Lint still clean (no banned directives introduced). Line count unchanged at 124.

diagnosis: INSTRUCTION-AUDIT POLICING — confirmed defect, NOT caught by the lint's first pass. Direct read of apollo_marketer_prompt.md lines 125-126 (the trailing STEP 4 / output-format section) found 2 banned directives seeded by the 2026-06-09 live test: (a) a call_cta 'If the reply is positive, propose a quick 15-minute Zoom call to walk them through Ralph Workflow and book it via your calendar link' — explicitly named in the prompt's banned pattern list (R3 '15-min call offer' mistake), and (b) a winner_claim 'If one variant shows a higher open rate than the other after the first day, declare it the winning angle and reallocate all remaining contacts to it' — explicitly named in EXPERIMENT DESIGN STANDARDS as a violation (winner claims at n<30, open-rate conclusions). Both directives are anti-patterns that would have surfaced the moment a positive reply arrived or a 24h open_rate window elapsed. The lint had not flagged them in its first run — re-ran the NEGATION-allowlist pass and confirmed the lint would NOT have caught them because they don't contain negation tokens. Caught by direct read. Surgical removal: deleted both lines, kept the surrounding STEP 4 ledger instruction + STOP line intact.
change_made: Removed lines 125-126 of agents/marketing/apollo_marketer_prompt.md. Both were 1-line directives at the end of STEP 4 (output format). After removal, file ends cleanly with the STEP 4 'Write one JSON line to tactic_ledger.jsonl' instruction and the STOP line. Re-ran apollo_prompt_lint.py — confirms clean ('no banned directives in instruction files'). File now 121 lines (was 123). Did NOT rewrite surrounding text, did NOT touch any other section. Did NOT weaken Guard H in apollo_activate_floor.py (Guard H is the line-of-defense against any future call-CTA creep).
hypothesis: If the 2 banned directives are removed, then the marketer will (a) NOT propose 15-min Zoom calls to positive replies (a contact-relationship breaker pre-first-conversation) and (b) NOT declare winning angles on Day-1 open rates (an over-confident n=2 conclusion that would be a process failure). Measurable: the next customer_discovery.jsonl entry (if a positive reply lands in 14d) will not include a '15-min call offered' line, and the next R4 24h read ledger entry will not include a 'winning angle declared' line. Anti-target: zero re-introductions of the banned phrases into any prompt/doc/template in the next 7d.

diagnosis: TUNING: addressed the activity-theater failure mode in the Day 14/21 LinkedIn-pivot path. The current R6 rule says 'if 0/9 by Day 21, pivot to LinkedIn DM' — but the prompt has no instruction to draft the LinkedIn DM template EARLIER. If the email experiment stalls at Day 14/21, the LinkedIn pivot is a multi-hour rewrite, and the moment of pivot is when the marketer is least likely to have the time/energy to build it. The 2026-06-09 R3 '15-min call offer' mistake is the same pattern: a fix that should have been pre-staged was left for the moment of need and produced a defective result. Pre-staging the LinkedIn DM template during the silent-experiment window turns the Day 21 pivot into a one-click activation, not a multi-hour build.

Save to agents/marketing/drafts/<date>_linkedin_dm_template.md. After that, only revise it if the positioning changes. If the email channel stalls at Day 14/21, the LinkedIn pivot is one-click, not a multi-hour rewrite.' Lint clean (no banned directives introduced). 1 tuning change only — no other protocol edits in this run. Line count 121 → 125 (now 4 lines longer).

diagnosis: COLD SWEEP — defect D17 confirmed via the absurdity-sweep step. The marketer prompt's DUTY 0 (warm pool first + pool discovery is the job) was being read on every run but the program did 0 warm-pool actions across 8+ evaluator runs while warm_pool.md grew from 0 → 14 NEW engagers. The 14 NEW engagers are stargazers/watchers who already raised their hand — highest-ROI surface, near-zero cost. The defect: the prompt's DUTY 0 language was 'every 🔥 NEW engager gets profiled THIS RUN' — profiling alone is not an action, and the prompt did not bind a follow-up step. This is the warm-pool version of D07 (arms-at-2-contacts); the activity-theater pattern applied to warm-pool: read the file, note the engagers, exit with no action. Other absurdity-sweep findings: (1) marconae GitHub engagement at #14 was just posted (the highest-leverage single action in the program; the organic-advocacy pattern that has actually moved stars); the program should not be auditing Apollo sequences at this moment. (2) Live state has V1=13 active, V3=13 active, V7=0 active — a 0-contact placeholder. The activation floor will self-correct by aborting V7 to enforce the 2-active cap (line 347-352 of apollo_activate_floor.py). (3) The 1-2 sends/day cap is the binding ramp constraint, but warm-pool work doesn't have a per-run send cap (it's GitHub/email, separate channels). The D17 defect is the highest-leverage fix.
change_made: REPAIR: (1) Appended D17 to APOLLO_DEFECT_CLASSES.md: 'Warm-pool duty is read but never bound to an action (the prompt's DUTY 0 says every 🔥 NEW engager gets profiled but does not require a concrete next-step per engager).' Principle: warm pool is the highest-ROI surface; profiling without action is the activity-theater pattern. (2) Added a 4-line BINDING block to the marketer prompt's DUTY 0: at least ONE 🔥 NEW engager per run must get a concrete next-step (profile + draft, 1-line reply on their issue/PR, or a logged reason for skipping). 'Profiling with no action' is NOT an action item. Re-ran prompt lint — clean. (3) Preserved the existing DUTY 0 language (priority ordering, three duties a/b/c, parallel-with-cold-pipeline) verbatim; the binding block is additive, not a rewrite. (4) Lint clean (no banned directives introduced). (5) Defect classes file grew 29→30 lines; prompt grew 125→131 lines.

hypothesis: If the warm-pool duty is binding (at least 1 NEW engager per run gets a concrete next-step), then by 2026-06-16 (7d checkback): the warm_pool.md file will be accompanied by at least 1 warm-pool action item per run (drafts/<date>_<user>.md, or a logged interaction, or a skip-with-reason). The 14 NEW engagers will drop to 0 unaddressed in 14 runs. Primary metric target: at least 1 customer_discovery.jsonl entry from a warm-pool source (vs the current pattern where all 5 validated learnings come from cold-discovery or GitHub). Anti-target: zero 'profile-only' warm-pool entries logged (would mean the D17 binding is being ignored). Secondary: the binding is read as a real constraint, not a checkbox — the action items should vary (sometimes a profile, sometimes a reply, sometimes a skip-with-reason) rather than a uniform 'profile 1 person' pattern.

diagnosis: COLD SWEEP — D25 confirmed via absurdity-sweep step. The scorecard headline was reporting '21 sent · 0 opens · 0 replies' when the live Apollo API shows unique_opened_unfiltered = 6 across V3+V9 (V3=5 on 16 delivered, V9=1 on 5 delivered). The scorecard read unique_opened (the MPP-FILTERED tracked count, often 0 because Apple Mail Privacy Protection pre-fetches every send and registers a 0-pixel open for tracking only) and reported it as the opens. The ground-truth human-open count lives in unique_opened_unfiltered. Result: a senior marketer reading the headline would conclude 'the angle is invisible' (the exact D6/D20 pattern in different clothing) and either kill the experiment or redesign the subject line — when the actual data is 27.3% raw open rate, the angle IS being read, the read-to-reply gap is the real diagnostic (R4 protocol territory). Same defect propagated to apollo_outcome_reader.py (the cron log line also reported '0op' instead of the real 6 unfiltered opens).

diagnosis: TUNING — R4 silence protocol was reading a single 'open_rate' number without distinguishing MPP-filtered from unfiltered. The protocol's trigger threshold was 'open_rate >30%' but the scorecard was reading the MPP-filtered count (often 0); the real raw open count for the current V3+V9 pair is 27.3% with 0 replies — exactly the 'being read but not replied' pattern that R4 is supposed to catch, but the threshold never fires at 27% raw. Worse: R4 had no concrete sharpen-copy step. The current state (raw-open>15%, 0 replies, n=22) is the textbook silent-experiment scenario and the protocol should prescribe ONE of: shorter subject, sharper Mom-Test question, lower recipient tier, or 1-sentence per-contact opener — not just 'pre-stage the next sequence'.

(b) Added a 4-line CONCRETE sharpen-copy action bullet specifying 4 named sharpen tactics (shorter subject to <36 chars, sharper Mom-Test question, lower recipient tier, 1-sentence per-contact opener) and the apollo_copy_sharpen ledger tag. (c) Kept the original 3 R4 bullets intact (the in-flight-data-is-not-conclusive guidance, the R8 fill-first principle, the pre-stage-next-sequence step). Surgical edit. Lint clean. Prompt grew 131 → 135 lines (+4 lines net for the sharpen-copy bullet, the open-rate reading expansion netted 0 because the original 'if open_rate >30%' was replaced by a more specific version).

diagnosis: COLD SWEEP — agency-ratio defect caught by inspecting /tmp + tactic_ledger. The marketer's earlier turn revealed 12 verified DevRels into /tmp/v9_topup_2.json at 06:13 (logged correctly in the per-reveal save) but the contact-creation step (POST /contacts × 12 + POST /emailer_campaigns/{v9_id}/add_contact_ids) never completed. V9 enrollment stayed at 21/30 for ~75 minutes. The scorecard showed 'V9 21/30 — underfilled, top up THIS RUN' but the marketer's process had stalled. Meanwhile the tactic_ledger had 0 marketer actions today (only 1 floor action: the R7 abort of V7), so the agency check would have read 'the floor is doing the marketing' even though the marketer was actually mid-process. ROOT CAUSE: the contact-creation two-step (POST /contacts → POST /emailer_campaigns/{id}/add_contact_ids) requires a non-obvious body field — `emailer_campaign_id` in the body, not just the URL. Without it, Apollo returns 422 'Please specify a emailer_campaign_id and send_email_from_email_account_id.' This is a 3rd instance of Apollo's confusing partial-path/partial-body schema (after the D16 cross-sequence re-enrollment block and the D23 fleet-monitor silent-skip pattern). D16 dedup check also applied: 2 of the 12 revealed (Jenna Pederson, Ricardo Ferreira) were already in V9 from the 1st morning batch.
